summary: Integrated ADR 0240 on top of repo release 0.177.64 by refreshing the protected release surfaces after Harbor advanced the prior mainline to 0.177.63, mirroring the exact repo-managed interactive ops portal service and data payload onto docker-runtime-lv3, rebuilding the ops-portal container, and re-verifying both the guest-local Apache ECharts panels and the authenticated public edge on ops.example.com.

verification.check: Focused repo validation and release preparation
verification.observed: `make preflight WORKFLOW=converge-ops-portal`, `make validate-data-models`, `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards`, `git diff --check`, `scripts/generate_adr_index.py --check`, `scripts/platform_manifest.py --check`, and `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema --with-requirements requirements/integration-tests.txt --with-requirements requirements/ops-portal.txt pytest tests/test_interactive_ops_portal.py tests/test_runtime_assurance_scoreboard.py tests/test_ops_portal.py` all passed from the rebased mainline candidate, and `scripts/release_manager.py --bump patch --dry-run` followed by the live run prepared release `0.177.64`.

The normal `make converge-ops-portal` wrapper, `make syntax-check-ops-portal`, and the equivalent direct mutation playbook launch from this Codex environment all received local exit `143` / signal `15` before `ansible-playbook` could emit its first task output, so the controller-local limitation was recorded instead of treating it as guest or syntax failure.

verification.check: Exact-main service and data payload replay
verification.observed: The integrated candidate payload was staged under `/tmp/ops-portal-mainline` on `docker-runtime-lv3`, promoted into `/opt/ops-portal/service` and `/opt/ops-portal/data` with `rsync` plus `install`, macOS metadata files were purged, and `docker compose up -d --build --remove-orphans` rebuilt `lv3-ops-portal:latest` and recreated the `ops-portal` container.

verification.check: Guest-local runtime verification after exact-main replay
verification.observed: Guest-local hashes for `/opt/ops-portal/service/ops_portal/app.py`, `static/portal.js`, and `templates/partials/overview.html` matched the repository files; `curl -fsS http://10.10.10.20:8092/health` returned `{"status":"ok"}`; the root page contained `https://unpkg.com/echarts@5.6.0/dist/echarts.min.js` plus the expected `data-echart-target` mounts; and `/partials/overview` included both `Runtime Assurance` and the ECharts mount points.

`curl -ks https://ops.example.com/health` returned `{"status":"ok"}` and `curl -k -I https://ops.example.com` returned `HTTP/2 302` to `/oauth2/sign_in?rd=https://ops.example.com/` while preserving the CSP allowance for `https://unpkg.com`.

notes: This mainline receipt supersedes the earlier branch-local ADR 0240 live-apply receipt as the canonical `ops_portal_visualizations` evidence on main.

The exact-main replay used the documented staged service/data promotion fallback because the local Codex execution environment terminates long-running mutation playbooks with signal 15 before the remote apply can emit task output.
The protected canonical-truth updates that follow this receipt record ADR 0240 in repo version 0.177.64 and platform version 0.130.46.
